# BattINFO

> The semantic data layer for battery technology: a shared EMMO-aligned vocabulary, ready-to-use JSON Schemas, JSON-LD output, persistent identifiers, and a Python library + CLI for authoring, validating, and publishing machine-readable battery records.

A BattINFO record is a canonical JSON object carrying exactly one top-level
discriminator key — `cell_spec`, `cell_instance`, `material`, `electrode`,
`electrolyte`, `test`, `dataset` (and more) — plus `schema_version`. It is
validated against canonical JSON Schemas and publishes to EMMO-aligned JSON-LD.
Every published record gets a persistent `w3id.org/battinfo/` IRI that content-
negotiates: `Accept: application/ld+json` or `text/turtle` returns machine data,
a browser gets HTML.

## Contracts
- JSON-LD @context (records): https://w3id.org/battinfo/context/records/v1.json
- Vocabulary (Turtle): https://w3id.org/battinfo/ns
- JSON Schemas (draft 2020-12): https://github.com/BIG-MAP/BattINFO/tree/main/assets/schemas — each declares `$id: https://w3id.org/battinfo/schema/{name}.schema.json`
- Validation contract (policies, structured issue codes, CLI/JSON output): https://big-map.github.io/BattINFO/dev/validation-contract.html
- Identifier policy (IRI minting, content negotiation, tombstones): https://big-map.github.io/BattINFO/dev/identifiers.html
- Schema + version reference: https://big-map.github.io/BattINFO/dev/pages/reference.html

## Examples
- Worked records (Python / canonical JSON / JSON-LD): https://battinfo.org/examples
- Raw JSON-LD artifacts: https://battinfo.org/jsonld/showcase-{slug}.jsonld (Content-Type: application/ld+json)

## Authoring and consumption
- The Python package is the SDK: https://github.com/BIG-MAP/BattINFO
- CLI verdicts match the browser and the registry gate (same schemas, drift-checked in CI): `battinfo validate --format json`
- Registry submission + listing API exposes an OpenAPI document at the service's `/openapi.json`.

## Notes for autonomous agents
- Type a record by its top-level discriminator key + `schema_version`; accept `0.1.0`, `1.0.0`, and `0.2.0`.
- Quantities are typed, never strings: `{"@type": [...], "hasNumericalPart": {"hasNumberValue": N}, "hasMeasurementUnit": "<EMMO unit IRI>"}`.
- During the soft launch, some record IRIs resolve to an auth-gated HTML landing page for browsers; machine content negotiation (`application/ld+json`, `text/turtle`) is unauthenticated.
